findings.title: Denied-path test asserts error.message only; expectedFailClosedOn reason codes are self-validated, not behavior-asserted
findings.description: assertPermissionAuditBindingDeniedPathCaseThrows checks error.message equals the expected string and, for forbidden_claim cases, cross-checks expectedReasonByForbiddenClaim[claim] against the case's expectedFailClosedOn (test data vs. test data). The thrown error's machine-readable fail-closed reason is never asserted, so a drift in the precheck's internal reason code (while the message stayed stable) would go undetected. Each message uniquely encodes the claim/reason so coverage is functionally adequate; the reason-code map is effectively documentation-only.
findings.recommendation: Optional: if the precheck attaches a structured reason (e.g., error.failClosedOn), assert it alongside the message so expectedFailClosedOn is behavior-verified rather than self-referential.

findings.title: Falsy-non-false fail-closed coverage limited to a single claim
findings.description: Only falsy_forbidden_claim.execute_retry_zero (execute_retry: 0) exercises rejection of a falsy-but-not-false forbidden value; the other 36 forbidden claims are tested only with true. Because the precheck applies one uniform presence-based check across all forbidden keys, a single falsy case is representative, but the falsy invariant is proven for one claim rather than the set.
findings.recommendation: Optional: leave as-is given uniform handling, or add a one-line comment noting all forbidden keys share one presence-based check so one falsy case suffices.

findings.title: unknown_claim type has no exposed sub-count; 37+4+1 sub-counts do not sum to deniedCaseCount 43
findings.description: deniedCaseCount=43 = 37 forbidden_claim + 4 input_drift + 1 claims_shape + 1 unknown_claim. Only forbiddenClaimCaseCount/inputDriftCaseCount/claimsShapeCaseCount are exposed and validated (sum 42); the unknown_claim is implicit in the total. deniedAssertions does include unknown_truthy_retry_claim_rejected, so the behavior is tested — this is a count-exposure clarity gap, not a correctness defect, and the RP validator checks each count independently so no contradiction exists.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add an unknownClaimCaseCount (mirrored in contract/fixture/validator) so all 43 cases are accounted for by explicit sub-counts.

security_notes: Denied matrix enforces fail-closed rejection of 37 forbidden claims spanning service execution, runtime route creation, DB/storage/product-state writes, runtime permission/authz/permission-engine/security-trimming, audit ledger/event writes, Claude review invocation, review/approval/human-approval/blocked-claim writes, rollback/retry/compensation execution, operator/customer notification, incident/observability writes, and real client/matter data usage — consistent with the test-only, no-write boundary.
Presence-based fail-closed is demonstrated: execute_retry: 0 (falsy, non-false) is rejected, not just truthy values; unknown truthy override (unreviewed_retry_override) is also rejected.

Input-drift cases (wrong S16 rollback marker, claude_review entrypoint, null rollback receipt, denied rollback decision) confirm upstream-chain validation; non-object claims are rejected (claims_must_be_object).
Fixture asserts synthetic_only / deterministic / tenant_scoped / no_real_client_data / no_real_matter_data / no_credentials_or_secrets; no secrets or real data present in the diff.
test_notes: Pre-review reports node --check = 0 across policies/service/validators/service.test/RP validator, and node --test packages/control-plane/test/service.test.js = 417 pass.

Only outstanding RP-validator failure pre-closeout is the not-yet-created CP00-016 evidence files (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-016); expected at this stage and resolved by pack assembly + adjudication. Implementation substance is internally consistent.

Cross-artifact consistency verified: contract control_plane_permission_audit_binding_unit_test_denied_path_definition, CONTROL_PLANE_..._UNIT_TEST_DENIED_PATH_POLICY/MODULE/CASE_IDS, fixture, service.test.js cases, and RP validator agree on subphase RP00.P02.M05.S20, testedSubphaseIds S01-S19 (19), denied counts 43/37/4/1, refs, gates, and handoff.

Denied matrix is adequate: 43 cases = 37 forbidden_claim + 1 unknown_claim + 4 input_drift + 1 claims_shape; valid retry-behavior baseline still passes (retry_behavior_decision=retry_behavior_metadata_only, source_micro_phase_completed=false, next_subphase=RP00.P02.M05.S19).

Closeout/handoff correct: S20 completes RP00.P02.M05 (completesSourceMicroPhase=true), weighted boundary is terminal with nextSubphaseId=null, and future handoff is RP00.P02.M06.S01 across policy/contract/fixture.

No false claims detected: noRealData=true, writesProductState=false, and all doesNot* safety flags=true accurately reflect a test-only, no-write subphase that exercises the pure precheckControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRetryBehavior function via assert.throws.
raw_review.summary: CP00-016 / RP00.P02.M05.S20 is a metadata-and-precheck denied-path unit test that closes micro-phase RP00.P02.M05. Contract definition, policy/module/case-id exports, synthetic fixture, service.test.js cases, and the RP contract validator are mutually consistent: 43 denied cases (37 forbidden_claim + 1 unknown_claim + 4 input_drift + 1 claims_shape) reject every unsafe runtime/write/permission/audit/review/approval/recovery/retry/rollback claim, plus falsy-non-false, unknown override, input drift, and non-object claims, while the valid retry-behavior baseline still passes. Safety flags (noRealData, writesProductState=false, all doesNot* true) are accurate, the weighted boundary is terminal, and handoff to RP00.P02.M06.S01 is declared across policy/contract/fixture. Pre-review reports node --check=0 (5 files), node --test=417 pass, and the RP validator clean except for the not-yet-created CP00-016 evidence files (expected pre-closeout). No P0/P1/P2 issues; three P3 quality notes only (reason-code assertion granularity, single falsy case, missing unknown_claim sub-count). The pack can close after evidence assembly and adjudication.

raw_review_text: Review complete. **PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — no blockers (0 P0/P1/P2, 3 P3 notes). CP00-016 / RP00.P02.M05.S20 is internally consistent across contract, policy/module, fixture, test, and RP validator; the 43-case denied matrix is comprehensive and fail-closed, safety flags are accurate, M05 completion + handoff to RP00.P02.M06.S01 are declared, and the only outstanding validator item is the not-yet-created evidence files (expected pre-closeout). The pack can close after evidence assembly and adjudication.
adjudication_notes: Claude returned PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with no P0, P1, or P2 findings. Three P3 findings are deferred in adjudication.md.
